title: santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. arrabidensis
intro: Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. arrabidensis, also known as Spanish chamomile or grey cotton lavender, is a subspecies of the Santolina rosmarinifolia plant, a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. arrabidensis include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep, and soothe digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. arrabidensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
